description_long: This internet cafe features a modular structure with blue partition walls, each adorned with the Southwest.com logo and branding. The building is organized into several cubicle-like sections, likely for individual computer stations, and is set on a gray rectangular floor base. At the front, three wooden desks with desktop computers and monitors are arranged in a row, providing a clear entry point for customers. The walls are made of lightweight panels, possibly metal or composite, and the desks appear to be made of wood with computer equipment on top. The overall design is functional and modern, with a focus on efficient use of space and clear branding. The interior is partially visible, showing the arrangement of workstations and the open entryway.
description: A modular blue-walled internet cafe with Southwest.com branding, featuring three front desks with computers and multiple interior cubicles. The design is modern and functional, emphasizing clear branding and efficient workstation layout.
description_view_0: Front view showing three wooden desks with computers, the main entry, and blue partition walls with Southwest.com logos.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the row of desks with computers and the length of the blue-walled cubicle structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the blue partition walls with branding, and no visible entry or desks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the arrangement of cubicles and the row of desks from behind.
attribute_signage: Blue panels display the Southwest.com logo and text in white and red, non-illuminated, placed on each partition wall in English.
attribute_computer_station_visibility: Three computer stations are clearly visible at the front, arranged in a row on wooden desks, each with a monitor and keyboard.
